sections.paragraph:
ἀναψύχω (s. ἀνάψυξις) fut. ἀναψύξω (JosAs 3:3); 1 aor. ἀνέψυξα; pf. ptc. acc. sg. ἀνεψυκότα 2 Macc 13:11; 1 aor. pass. ptc. ἀναψυχθείς (TestSol 4:11 D).

① trans. (Hom.+; Plut.; TestSol 4:11 D; Jos., Ant. 15, 54. [Nägeli 16; Anz 303]) to provide relief from obligation or trouble, give someone a breathing space, revive, refresh τινά IEph 2:1; Tr 12:2. πολλάκις με ἀνέψυξεν he often refreshed me 2 Ti 1:16.

② intr. (Diphilus [c. 300 b.c.] 81; POslo 153, 10 [early II a.d.]; POxy 1296, 7; Sb 3939, 28, in a letter on a wood tablet fr. a mummy requesting someth. for ‘repose’; cp. JSvennung, Aretos, Acta Philol. Fenn., n.s. II, ’58, 183; also 1 Km 16:23; Ps 38:14; En 103:13; JosAs 3:3; ParJer 6:7; Babrius 95, 57) to experience relief from obligation or trouble, be refreshed μετά τινος together with someone Ro 15:32 D.—DELG s.v. ψυχρός C1 ψύχω 1295. M-M. TW. Spicq.

fut. fut. = fut.

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. pf. = perfect

ptc. ptc. = participle

acc. acc. = accusative

  1. sg. = singular

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

trans. trans. = transitive

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Anz Anz = HA., Subsidia—List 6

IEph IEph = Ignatius to the Ephesians—List 1

intr. intr. = intransitive

POslo POslo = Papyri Osloenses—List 4

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

  1. fr. = from

someth. someth. = something

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Philol Philol , Zeitschrift für das klassische Altertum—List 6

n.s. n.s. = new series (also used to render Neue Folge)

En En = I Enoch—List 2

ParJer ParJer = Paraleipomena Jeremiou, II? a.d.—List 2

Babrius Babrius , c. 200 a.d.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 75
